name: Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Proserpina y Acueducto de los Miraglos — circular desde Mérida
description: Cycle the Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop, a moderate 13.6-mile route exploring Roman history and Extremadura's landscapes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mérida traverse a landscape rich in historical Roman heritage and natural features. The region is characterized by ancient Roman aqueducts, serene reservoirs, and natural parks, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Trails often follow river paths and historical routes like the Vía de la Plata, providing a mix of flat sections and gentle inclines. This area provides a blend of cultural exploration and outdoor activity for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Mérida The most popular touring cycling route is Cornalvo Park and Reservoirs Loop, a 27.6 miles (44.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park and its reservoirs, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop, a moderate 13.6 miles (21.8 km) path. This trail combines ancient Roman roads with views of the Proserpina Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Guadiana Natural Trail and Vía de la Plata Loop, a 14.2 miles (22.8 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and historical paths, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Touring cycling around Mérida is defined by ancient Roman structures, natural trails along rivers, and tranquil reservoir paths. The network offers options for easy touring bike trails Merida, as well as moderate routes for more experienced cyclists. The routes in Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mérida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mérida?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Mérida, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Mérida?
A: Yes, Mérida offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021486618/guadiana-natural-trail-and-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.2-mile (22.8 km) trail that follows riverine landscapes and historical paths, ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Mérida?
A: The terrain around Mérida is varied, characterized by ancient Roman aqueducts, serene reservoirs, and natural parks. Routes often follow river paths and historical routes like the Vía de la Plata, providing a mix of flat sections and gentle inclines. You'll find a blend of cultural exploration and outdoor activity.

Q: Are there any advanced touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced cyclists, Mérida offers 15 difficult routes. While specific advanced routes are not detailed here, the region's diverse landscape ensures challenging options for those seeking more demanding rides.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Mérida's routes are rich in historical and natural sights. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Monuments of Mérida&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Mérida&lt;/a&gt; is another notable landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mérida?
A: Mérida experiences hot summers, so spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable rides and allowing you to fully enjoy the historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Mérida?
A: Yes, many routes around Mérida are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Los Milagros Aqueduct loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a 43.2 km circular journey, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Mérida?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the blend of ancient Roman structures, natural trails along rivers, and tranquil reservoir paths.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Mérida typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021459255/cornalvo-park-and-reservoirs-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Park and Reservoirs Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 27.6 miles (44.4 km), typically takes around 3 hours 43 minutes, while shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Roman Dam of Proserpina loop&lt;/a&gt; (20.1 km) can be completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that combine historical Roman roads with natural landscapes?
A: Absolutely. Mérida is renowned for its Roman heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021463242/via-de-la-plata-and-proserpina-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, combining sections of the ancient Roman road with scenic views of the Proserpina Reservoir.

Q: What should I pack for a touring cycling trip in Mérida?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for warm weather, it's advisable to pack essentials like sufficient water, sun protection (sunscreen, hat), basic bike repair tools, and appropriate cycling attire. Depending on the season, light layers are recommended. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.
tips: A Roman relic that is still in use. Construction of the reservoir began in the 1st century BC. to supply Mérida through its aqueduct and is still active today as a bathing and recreation area. This thousand-year-old structure is part of the civil engineering complex that completes the majestic monuments that adorn the cities and is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Beautiful area where water was collected by the Romans and guided to Merida

This colossal aqueduct is part of a hydraulic pipeline that brought water from the Proserpina reservoir or Charca de la Albuera. It is popularly known as "Los Milagros" due to the admiration that its state of conservation caused in locals and foreigners despite the vicissitudes of time. Awesome place!

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop typically takes around 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete. It covers a distance of approximately 21.8 kilometers (13.5 miles).

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop is rated as moderate. While the Proserpina Reservoir section is generally easy and suitable for all skill levels, the integration with the Vía de la Plata adds some rolling hills and varied terrain, requiring good basic fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the loop?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, including sections of gravel, asphalt, concrete, and dirt. The Proserpina Reservoir part is often on paved or well-maintained paths, while the Vía de la Plata sections can be more mixed. A hybrid or gravel bike is recommended over a road bike due to the varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop features significant historical landmarks. You'll pass the impressive Roman Dam of Proserpina, which is still in use today, and the ancient Acueducto de los Milagros. The route also includes the scenic Proserpina Reservoir Route itself.

faqs.question: Is the Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The Proserpina Reservoir section is an easy 10 km route, perfect for families and beginners. However, the full 21.8 km loop, incorporating parts of the Vía de la Plata, is rated moderate due to its length and rolling hills. Beginners with some cycling experience should be fine, but families with very young children might prefer to stick to just the reservoir section.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this loop is during early spring or late autumn. Summers in Extremadura can be extremely hot, especially in the south, making cycling less comfortable. Spring and autumn offer more pleasant temperatures for touring.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop. The route uses public paths and roads.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to access the loop near Mérida?
faqs.answer: You can find parking in Mérida, which is just 4 to 5 kilometers from the Proserpina Reservoir. From Mérida, it's an easy ride to connect to the reservoir loop. Look for public parking areas within the city that are convenient for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is the Vía de la Plata and Proserpina Reservoir Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, dogs are generally welcome on cycling routes in Spain, provided they are kept on a leash and under control. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the cleanliness of the area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop integrates with sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. Specifically, it covers parts of the 'Mtb Vía de la Plata' and 'Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca' official trails.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: Near the Proserpina Reservoir, especially during summer, you can find outdoor cafés. In Mérida, a key city on the Vía de la Plata, you'll find a full range of services including accommodation, workshops, cafes, and restrooms.
